meta.name: RAADS-14 Autism Spectrum Screening Scale
meta.description: The RAADS-14 is a 14-item self-report screening scale for assessing autism spectrum traits in adults, covering social interaction, language, sensorimotor, and circumscribed interests.
meta.author: Eriksson, Andersen & Bejerot (2013)
meta.reference: Eriksson JM, Andersen LM, Bejerot S. RAADS-14 Screen: validity of a screening tool for autism spectrum disorder in an adult psychiatric population. Molecular Autism. 2013;4(1):49.
meta.instruction: Please select the option that best describes your experience. Some items ask you to distinguish between childhood (before age 16) and current experience.

commonChoices.label: True only when I was younger than 16
True now and when I was young

questions.text: It is difficult for me to understand how other people are feeling when we are talking
I often use words and phrases from movies or TV in conversation
I am often surprised when others tell me I have been insensitive or rude
Sometimes I talk too loudly or too softly, and I am not aware of it
I often don't know how to act in social situations
I can 'put myself in other people's shoes'
I have a hard time figuring out what some phrases mean, like 'you are the apple of my eye'
I only like to talk to people who share my special interests
I focus on details rather than the overall idea
I always notice how food feels in my mouth. This is more important to me than how it tastes
I miss my best friends or family when we are apart for a long time
Sometimes I offend others by saying what I am thinking, even if I don't mean to
I only like to think and talk about a few things that interest me
I'd rather go out to eat in a restaurant by myself than with someone I know

ranges.label: Below Threshold
ranges.description: Your score is below the autism spectrum screening threshold. It is unlikely that you have autism spectrum disorder.
suggestions: This result is only a preliminary screening
If you have concerns in other areas, consider consulting a professional

ranges.label: Moderate ASD Traits
ranges.description: Your score exceeds the screening threshold, suggesting some autism spectrum traits. This does not mean a diagnosis — further professional assessment is recommended.
suggestions: Consider seeking a professional neurodevelopmental assessment
Explore social and communication strategies that work for you
Joining a support community may be helpful

ranges.label: Elevated ASD Traits
ranges.description: Your score is elevated, suggesting notable autism spectrum traits. A professional diagnostic assessment is strongly recommended.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to seek a professional autism spectrum assessment
A formal diagnosis must be made by a qualified clinician
Receiving a diagnosis can help you understand yourself and access support
Learn about the concept of neurodiversity
